input.title: Using % formatting
input.code:
name = "Alice"
age = 30
message = "Hello %s, you are %d years old" % (name, age)

output.technical: f-strings (literal string interpolation) introduced in Python 3.6 evaluate expressions at runtime in C and are faster and more readable than `%` formatting or `.format()`.
output.beginner: Using f-strings is the modern, fastest way to plug variables into text.
output.analogy: It's like using a modern digital printer instead of a manual typewriter.
output.solution: Use f-strings: `f"Hello {name}, you are {age} years old"`.
output.prevention: Adopt f-strings as the default string formatting method in Python.
output.optimized_code:
name = "Alice"
age = 30
message = f"Hello {name}, you are {age} years old"
